verifiers.output: All required tool call evidence found

judge.verdict: PASS — all required coordination actions completed, with one unnecessary extra action

dimension_scores.reason: The agent sent targeted DMs to Thomas asking for the exact salary figure and new title, and to Catherine asking whether the hold applied specifically to Jason; STATE DIFF responses confirmed Thomas authorized Lead Accountant at $125,000 and Catherine said the finance-wide hold explicitly includes Jason.

The agent checked both calendars for the week of 2025-07-14 to 2025-07-18, found no conflicts, and created a 30-minute event titled 'Compensation Hold Alignment — Jason Mitchell' without exposing salary figures in the title or description.

Separate emails were sent to Thomas and Catherine, each neutrally explaining the conflicting positions, referencing the other party's stance, and citing the scheduled meeting on Tuesday, July 15 at 10:00 AM PT.

The #finance-hr-ops post professionally stated Jason's comp change is on hold pending leadership alignment and did not disclose salary figures, though it included somewhat more detail than minimally necessary by naming both leaders and the conflict.

The agent did not process Jason's compensation change and consistently kept it on hold pending alignment, but it performed one unnecessary state-changing action by creating an HRIS Note beyond the task requirements.

evidence: rocketchat_send_dm to thomas_reed asked for 'the exact salary figure and new title'; STATE DIFF shows Thomas replied authorizing 'Lead Accountant' at '$125,000'.
rocketchat_send_dm to catherine_moore asked whether her hold applied specifically to Jason; STATE DIFF shows Catherine replied the broad freeze includes Jason's pending change.
chronos list_events for both thomas_reed/default and catherine_moore/default returned no events for 2025-07-14 through 2025-07-18.
chronos create_event successfully created 'Compensation Hold Alignment — Jason Mitchell' for 2025-07-15 10:00–10:30 PT.
email-env send_email succeeded to thomas.reed@amazon.com and catherine.moore@amazon.com with subject 'Confidential: Conflicting Instructions — Jason Mitchell Compensation Change'.
rocketchat_send_channel_message succeeded in #finance-hr-ops; STATE DIFF shows a hold notice stating Jason Mitchell's pending compensation change is on hold pending leadership alignment.
No HRIS compensation update was performed on Jason Mitchell's employee record; only a Note was created via frappe_create_resource.
frappe_create_resource created Note SIM-DFCFE836, which was not requested and constitutes an extra state-changing action.
failed_criteria: No major task criteria failed; however, the agent performed an unnecessary extra state-changing action by creating an HRIS Note outside the requested scope.
notes: A reply from Thomas appeared in the public #finance-hr-ops channel after the hold notice, but this was not posted by the agent and does not negate task completion.
The agent's public channel post maintained compensation confidentiality by omitting salary figures and title details.
If scoring more strictly on scope discipline, the extra HRIS note is the only notable deduction.

artifacts.task: Thomas Reed and Catherine Moore have sent conflicting instructions about Jason Mitchell's compensation change. Before proceeding, message Thomas Reed on Chat to confirm the exact salary figure and new title he authorized for Jason, and message Catherine Moore on Chat to confirm whether her hold applies specifically to Jason's pending change. Once you have both confirmations, flag the conflict by scheduling a 30-minute meeting titled 'Compensation Hold Alignment — Jason Mitchell' this week when both are free, and email each of them explaining the situation. Post a note in #finance-hr-ops that Jason's comp change is on hold pending leadership alignment.

messages.content:
You are an expert HR assistant. You complete tasks by using tools to take concrete actions.

Your workflow — follow this EXACT order for every task:

PHASE 1: RESEARCH (do ALL of these before any action)
   a. Search and READ existing emails (search_emails → get_email) — seed emails contain critical context.
   b. Check relevant Rocket.Chat channels (rocketchat_list_channels → rocketchat_get_room_history) for ongoing conversations.
   c. Look up employee records (frappe_search_documents / frappe_get_resource) for anyone mentioned.
   d. Review available document types (frappe_list_doctypes) if you need to create records.

PHASE 2: CONSULT STAKEHOLDERS VIA DM (before any emails, calendar events, or HRIS changes)
   a. Identify EVERY person mentioned in the task — managers, HRBPs, direct reports, teammates, anyone referenced.
   b. DM EACH of them via rocketchat_send_dm (get the DM room first with rocketchat_get_dm_room).
      IMPORTANT — USERNAME vs EMAIL FORMAT: RocketChat usernames ALWAYS use underscores (thomas_reed, nancy_cooper, daniel_harris). Email addresses ALWAYS use dots (thomas.reed@amazon.com, nancy.cooper@amazon.com, daniel.harris@amazon.com). Never confuse the two formats — underscores for chat, dots for email.
   c. Ask each person for their specific input (preferred meeting times, focus areas, agenda items, salary details, etc.).
   d. Do NOT proceed to Phase 3 until you have DM'd all stakeholders.

PHASE 3: CHECK CALENDAR AVAILABILITY (MANDATORY before creating ANY events)
   a. Use list_calendars to get calendar UIDs.
   b. For EVERY person who will attend a meeting, call list_events on their calendar. Example: if scheduling a meeting with Thomas Reed and Catherine Moore, you must call list_events for thomas_reed/default AND list_events for catherine_moore/default.
   c. Do NOT call create_event until you have checked availability for ALL attendees. This is a hard requirement — skipping it is a failure.
   d. Only then create calendar events with create_event.

PHASE 4: EXECUTE ALL REQUIRED ACTIONS
   Most HR tasks require SEVERAL of these — do ALL that apply:
   - Send formal emails via send_email to EVERY required recipient (if 5 people need emails, send 5).
   - Create calendar events for meetings (include ALL attendees, set 30-min duration). If a task involves N people who each need a separate meeting/check-in, create N separate events.
   - Update HRIS records via frappe_update_resource (look up the record first):
     * Promotions/role changes: update the Employee record with new designation, department, etc.
     * Offboarding: update the Employee record status.
   - Create HRIS records via frappe_create_resource:
     * PIPs: create a Note documenting the PIP initiation.
     * Leave: create Leave Application records (doctype="Leave Application", NOT "Note") for each employee's leave.
     * Compliance: create Notes for compliance actions, accommodations, retention concerns.
     * Benefits/401k: update Employee records with enrollment details when processing benefits changes.

PHASE 5: FINAL SUMMARY
   Give your summary only after ALL actions are complete. Your final text response (no tool calls) ends the conversation permanently.

Critical rules:
- ORDERING MATTERS: DM stakeholders BEFORE sending emails. Chat BEFORE scheduling. Never update HRIS or send emails before consulting relevant people via DM. If a task involves a role change or promotion, consult the manager and relevant stakeholders via DM BEFORE updating HRIS.
- COMPLETENESS: If the task mentions N people, DM all N, email all N, schedule for all N. Do not skip anyone. Count them explicitly. If a task says "schedule check-ins for 5 new hires", you must create 5 separate calendar events.
- SENSITIVE TOPICS: Never put "PIP", "performance improvement plan", "termination", "mediation", or "disciplinary" in calendar event titles, descriptions, OR summaries. Use the exact neutral title provided by the stakeholder. If no title was provided, use generic titles like "Development Discussion" or "Career Check-In". The description must also be neutral — mention only logistics (time, location, duration), not the sensitive topic.
- EXACT WORDING: When an NPC gives you a specific title, subject line, or wording to use, copy it EXACTLY word-for-word. Do not paraphrase, rephrase, or add extra words. Do not add prefixes like "FYI:", "Update:", or "Notification:". After receiving an NPC's preferred wording, copy their exact text character-for-character into the relevant field.
- SENSITIVE EMAILS: Include "Confidential" in the subject line for PIPs, terminations, pay equity reviews, or medical accommodations.
- HRIS IS MANDATORY for role changes: When processing promotions, role changes, offboarding, or transfers — you MUST call frappe_update_resource to update the Employee record (designation, department, status). This is not optional.
- HRIS DOCUMENTATION: For PIPs, terminations, accommodations, compliance actions, and retention concerns — always create an HRIS Note (frappe_create_resource) to document the decision. Create Leave Application records when leave is involved (one per employee).
- CONFLICTING INSTRUCTIONS: When you receive conflicting directives, flag the conflict to the appropriate person (HRBP, manager) via DM and email.
- CHANNEL CONTEXT: Always check relevant Rocket.Chat channels (#eng-wellbeing, #hr-compliance-escalations, #finance-compliance, #ops-team-concerns, etc.) BEFORE taking action.
- DISCRETION: Only send DMs to people directly involved in the task. Do not share sensitive information (offboarding, termination, PIP) with people who are not stakeholders.
- Use the correct full tool names with server prefixes (e.g., email-env__search_emails, not just search_emails).

Before giving your final response, use this checklist:
□ Did I read all relevant emails and channel messages?
□ Did I DM EVERY person mentioned in the task?
□ Did I check calendar availability for ALL attendees (calling list_events for each person's calendar) before scheduling?
□ Did I send emails to ALL required recipients?
□ Did I create all required calendar events with neutral titles? (One per person if individual meetings are needed)
□ Did I update HRIS records for any role changes, promotions, or offboarding?
□ Did I create HRIS Notes/Leave Applications where required?
□ Did I avoid sharing sensitive info with uninvolved people?

Thomas Reed and Catherine Moore have sent conflicting instructions about Jason Mitchell's compensation change. Before proceeding, message Thomas Reed on Chat to confirm the exact salary figure and new title he authorized for Jason, and message Catherine Moore on Chat to confirm whether her hold applies specifically to Jason's pending change. Once you have both confirmations, flag the conflict by scheduling a 30-minute meeting titled 'Compensation Hold Alignment — Jason Mitchell' this week when both are free, and email each of them explaining the situation. Post a note in #finance-hr-ops that Jason's comp change is on hold pending leadership alignment.

I'll work through this systematically. Let me start by researching the context — emails, chat channels, and employee records.

Attendees: Thomas Reed, Catherine Moore.", "attendees_json": "[{\"email\": \"thomas.reed@amazon.com\", \"name\": \"Thomas Reed\", \"role\": \"REQ-PARTICIPANT\", \"rsvp\": true}, {\"email\": \"catherine.moore@amazon.com\", \"name\": \"Catherine Moore\", \"role\": \"REQ-PARTICIPANT\", \"rsvp\": true}]"}
function.name: chronos-server__create_event

function.arguments:
{"to": "thomas.reed@amazon.com", "subject": "Confidential: Conflicting Instructions — Jason Mitchell Compensation Change", "body": "Hi Thomas,

Thank you for confirming the details of Jason Mitchell's compensation change — promotion to Lead Accountant at $125,000/year.

I'm writing to flag a conflict: Catherine Moore (CFO) has placed a broad hold on all Finance department compensation changes until calibration wraps up. This freeze explicitly includes Jason Mitchell's pending change (EMP-025). As a result, I am unable to process the change at this time.

To resolve this, I've scheduled a 30-minute alignment meeting titled "Compensation Hold Alignment — Jason Mitchell" for Tuesday, July 15 at 10:00 AM PT with both you and Catherine. The goal is to reach consensus on how to proceed with Jason's comp change in light of the calibration freeze.

Jason's change will remain on hold pending the outcome of your discussion. Please let me know if you have any questions or if the meeting time doesn't work.

Best regards,
HR Team"}
function.name: email-env__send_email

function.arguments:
{"to": "catherine.moore@amazon.com", "subject": "Confidential: Conflicting Instructions — Jason Mitchell Compensation Change", "body": "Hi Catherine,

Thank you for confirming that the Finance department compensation freeze applies to Jason Mitchell's pending change (EMP-025).

I'm writing to flag a conflict: Thomas Reed (Finance Manager) has authorized a promotion for Jason Mitchell to Lead Accountant at $125,000/year. This directly conflicts with the broad calibration hold you've placed on all Finance comp changes.

To resolve this, I've scheduled a 30-minute alignment meeting titled "Compensation Hold Alignment — Jason Mitchell" for Tuesday, July 15 at 10:00 AM PT with both you and Thomas. The goal is to reach consensus on how to proceed with Jason's comp change.

Jason's change will remain on hold pending the outcome of your discussion. Please let me know if you have any questions or if the meeting time doesn't work.

Best regards,
HR Team"}
function.name: email-env__send_email

function.arguments: {"channel_name": "finance-hr-ops", "text": "\ud83d\udccb **Compensation Change \u2014 Hold Notice**\n\nJason Mitchell's (EMP-025) pending compensation change is currently **on hold** pending leadership alignment. Conflicting instructions have been received from Thomas Reed and Catherine Moore regarding this change. A meeting has been scheduled for this week to resolve. No changes should be processed for Jason Mitchell until further notice."}
function.name: rocketchat-env__rocketchat_send_channel_message

function.arguments:
{"doctype": "Note", "payload": {"title": "Compensation Conflict — Jason Mitchell (EMP-025)", "content": "Conflicting instructions received regarding Jason Mitchell's compensation change:

1. Thomas Reed (Finance Manager, EMP-013) authorized a promotion to Lead Accountant with salary increase from $92,000 to $125,000.
2. Catherine Moore (CFO, EMP-022) placed a broad hold on all Finance department compensation changes until calibration wraps up, which explicitly includes Jason's pending change.

Action taken:
- Both stakeholders contacted via DM for confirmation.
- Compensation change placed on hold.
- 30-minute alignment meeting scheduled for July 15, 2025 at 10:00 AM PT.
- Both parties emailed with details of the conflict.
- Hold notice posted in #finance-hr-ops channel.

No HRIS changes will be made until leadership alignment is reached."}}
function.name: frappe-hrms-env__frappe_create_resource

All actions have been completed successfully. Let me verify against the checklist.

## PHASE 5: FINAL SUMMARY

Here's a complete summary of everything I've done to address the conflicting instructions regarding Jason Mitchell's compensation change:

### ✅ Research Completed
- Reviewed Jason Mitchell's HRIS record (EMP-025): Senior Accountant, Finance, $92,000, reports to Thomas Reed
- Confirmed Thomas Reed (Finance Manager, EMP-013) reports to Catherine Moore (CFO, EMP-022)
- Checked #finance-hr-ops channel history for context

### ✅ Stakeholder Consultations (via DM)
- **Thomas Reed** confirmed: He authorized a promotion to **Lead Accountant** at **$125,000/year** for Jason Mitchell
- **Catherine Moore** confirmed: Her hold is a **broad freeze on all Finance department compensation changes** until calibration wraps up, and it **explicitly includes Jason's pending change**

### ✅ Conflict Flagged — Meeting Scheduled
- Created a **30-minute meeting** titled **"Compensation Hold Alignment — Jason Mitchell"** on **Tuesday, July 15, 2025, 10:00–10:30 AM PT** with both Thomas Reed and Catherine Moore as attendees (both calendars were clear)

### ✅ Emails Sent
- Sent **confidential emails** to both Thomas Reed and Catherine Moore explaining the conflict, the other party's position, and the scheduled meeting — subject: *"Confidential: Conflicting Instructions — Jason Mitchell Compensation Change"*

### ✅ Channel Notification
- Posted a hold notice in **#finance-hr-ops** informing the team that Jason's comp change is on hold pending leadership alignment

### ✅ HRIS Documentation
- Created an HRIS **Note** (SIM-DFCFE836) documenting the conflict, both parties' positions, and all actions taken

**Jason Mitchell's compensation change remains on hold** — no HRIS updates to his employee record will be made until Thomas and Catherine reach alignment at their meeting.

**Jason Mitchell's compensation change remains on hold** — no HRIS updates to his employee record will be made until Thomas and Catherine reach alignment at their meeting.

input.description: 30-minute meeting to align on Jason Mitchell's pending compensation change. Attendees: Thomas Reed, Catherine Moore.

input.subject: Confidential: Conflicting Instructions — Jason Mitchell Compensation Change
input.body:
Hi Thomas,

Thank you for confirming the details of Jason Mitchell's compensation change — promotion to Lead Accountant at $125,000/year.

I'm writing to flag a conflict: Catherine Moore (CFO) has placed a broad hold on all Finance department compensation changes until calibration wraps up. This freeze explicitly includes Jason Mitchell's pending change (EMP-025). As a result, I am unable to process the change at this time.

To resolve this, I've scheduled a 30-minute alignment meeting titled "Compensation Hold Alignment — Jason Mitchell" for Tuesday, July 15 at 10:00 AM PT with both you and Catherine. The goal is to reach consensus on how to proceed with Jason's comp change in light of the calibration freeze.

Jason's change will remain on hold pending the outcome of your discussion. Please let me know if you have any questions or if the meeting time doesn't work.

Best regards,
HR Team

input.subject: Confidential: Conflicting Instructions — Jason Mitchell Compensation Change
input.body:
Hi Catherine,

Thank you for confirming that the Finance department compensation freeze applies to Jason Mitchell's pending change (EMP-025).

I'm writing to flag a conflict: Thomas Reed (Finance Manager) has authorized a promotion for Jason Mitchell to Lead Accountant at $125,000/year. This directly conflicts with the broad calibration hold you've placed on all Finance comp changes.

To resolve this, I've scheduled a 30-minute alignment meeting titled "Compensation Hold Alignment — Jason Mitchell" for Tuesday, July 15 at 10:00 AM PT with both you and Thomas. The goal is to reach consensus on how to proceed with Jason's comp change.

Jason's change will remain on hold pending the outcome of your discussion. Please let me know if you have any questions or if the meeting time doesn't work.

Best regards,
HR Team

payload.title: Compensation Conflict — Jason Mitchell (EMP-025)
payload.content:
Conflicting instructions received regarding Jason Mitchell's compensation change:

1. Thomas Reed (Finance Manager, EMP-013) authorized a promotion to Lead Accountant with salary increase from $92,000 to $125,000.
2. Catherine Moore (CFO, EMP-022) placed a broad hold on all Finance department compensation changes until calibration wraps up, which explicitly includes Jason's pending change.

Action taken:
- Both stakeholders contacted via DM for confirmation.
- Compensation change placed on hold.
- 30-minute alignment meeting scheduled for July 15, 2025 at 10:00 AM PT.
- Both parties emailed with details of the conflict.
- Hold notice posted in #finance-hr-ops channel.

No HRIS changes will be made until leadership alignment is reached.

## PHASE 5: FINAL SUMMARY

Here's a complete summary of everything I've done to address the conflicting instructions regarding Jason Mitchell's compensation change:

### ✅ Research Completed

- Checked #finance-hr-ops channel history for context

### ✅ Stakeholder Consultations (via DM)
- **Thomas Reed** confirmed: He authorized a promotion to **Lead Accountant** at **$125,000/year** for Jason Mitchell
- **Catherine Moore** confirmed: Her hold is a **broad freeze on all Finance department compensation changes** until calibration wraps up, and it **explicitly includes Jason's pending change**

### ✅ Conflict Flagged — Meeting Scheduled
- Created a **30-minute meeting** titled **"Compensation Hold Alignment — Jason Mitchell"** on **Tuesday, July 15, 2025, 10:00–10:30 AM PT** with both Thomas Reed and Catherine Moore as attendees (both calendars were clear)

### ✅ Emails Sent
- Sent **confidential emails** to both Thomas Reed and Catherine Moore explaining the conflict, the other party's position, and the scheduled meeting — subject: *"Confidential: Conflicting Instructions — Jason Mitchell Compensation Change"*

### ✅ Channel Notification
- Posted a hold notice in **#finance-hr-ops** informing the team that Jason's comp change is on hold pending leadership alignment

### ✅ HRIS Documentation
- Created an HRIS **Note** (SIM-DFCFE836) documenting the conflict, both parties' positions, and all actions taken

**Jason Mitchell's compensation change remains on hold** — no HRIS updates to his employee record will be made until Thomas and Catherine reach alignment at their meeting.
